title: Hepetis laxissima
taxonomy: Hepetis laxissima is a flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Hebe, which comprises over 100 species of flowering plants native to New Zealand and surrounding islands. The classification of Hebe laxissima is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Lamiales, Family: Plantaginaceae, Genus: Hebe, Species: H. laxissima. Hebe laxissima is a shrub or small tree with fragrant leaves and white or pink flowers. It is native to New Zealand and is often cultivated for its ornamental value.
